findings.title: Hysteresis band width (enter_probability vs exit_probability) is never isolated by a test
findings.description: CpaEvaluator's risk state uses two thresholds: a track enters risk at p>=enter_probability but only clears at p<exit_probability (line 36-38), which is the whole point of the enter/exit config pair. The existing unit tests cover Entered (EnteredFiresOnHighRisk), no-refire-while-risky, and Exited-via-deletion (DeletedTrackFiresExited). The full-stack integration test covers a probability-driven Exit, but in that head-on pass p collapses from ~1 straight through the band to ~0, so it never distinguishes exit_probability from enter_probability. No test drives p into the band (exit_probability <= p < enter_probability) after entering and asserts the pair stays Entered (held), nor asserts it does NOT re-enter after dropping just below enter.
findings.why_it_matters: If the exit comparison silently regressed to use enter_probability (collapsing the hold band to zero), every current test still passes, yet a target loitering at the alarm boundary would flap Entered/Exited every cycle — churning the operator collision-risk feed. Hysteresis is a documented safety-relevant behavior with a dedicated config knob whose effect is unverified.
findings.suggested_test: Construct a scene where computeCpaWithUncertainty yields p in (exit_probability, enter_probability): after an initial Entered, hold p in the band across several evaluate() cycles and assert no Exited and no new Entered; then push p below exit_probability and assert exactly one Exited. Also assert a track that only reaches the band (never enter_probability) never fires Entered.

findings.title: StaticHazardEvaluator emit_updates / Updated transition has zero coverage
findings.description: StaticHazardEvaluatorConfig.emit_updates gates a per-cycle Updated event while own-ship stays inside the keep-clear ring (evaluate() line 44-47). grep across tests/ shows emit_updates and CollisionRiskTransition::Updated are only exercised for CpaEvaluator (test_cpa_evaluator.cpp:157,165); StaticHazardTransition::Updated appears in no test. tests/collision/test_static_hazard_evaluator.cpp only covers Entered/Exited/hysteresis/null-sink/colocated. The config knob is default-off and completely untested.
findings.why_it_matters: A consumer that enables emit_updates to get continuous keep-clear proximity while looping inside a hazard ring relies on this branch. Silent breakage (branch never taken, or firing Entered/Exited instead of Updated) ships a wrong static-hazard event stream and nothing catches it.
findings.suggested_test: Set cfg.emit_updates=true, drive own-ship to enter and then dwell inside the ring for several evaluate() cycles; assert exactly one Entered followed by one Updated per subsequent in-ring cycle (with correct distance_m/keep_clear_m payload), then one Exited on leaving.

findings.title: position_uncertainty_m expansion of the hard-suppression core is untested
findings.description: birthSuppression computes r_hard = footprint_radius_m + position_uncertainty_m (line 61-62), so a charted obstacle with position uncertainty gets a larger c=1.0 hard core. Every test in test_static_obstacle_model.cpp (and the integration test) uses obstacles with position_uncertainty_m at its default 0, so the '+ position_uncertainty_m' term is never exercised; the ramp/clamp/datum tests would all pass even if that term were dropped.
findings.why_it_matters: The hard core is the region where a phantom birth is fully suppressed. If the uncertainty term silently regressed, an obstacle charted with large position uncertainty would present a too-small hard core, letting phantom/duplicate tracks be born in the uncertain annulus around it — an ADR-0002-relevant suppression failure that no test would flag.
findings.suggested_test: Build a StaticObstacleModel with footprint_radius_m small but position_uncertainty_m large; assert birthSuppression==1.0 at a distance between footprint_radius_m and footprint_radius_m+position_uncertainty_m (inside the expanded hard core), and that it drops below 1.0 just beyond r_hard.

findings.title: COG wrap into [0,360) for south/west-bound velocities is untested
findings.description: toVelocityOutput maps velocity to COG via atan2(v_east,v_north) then wraps negative results with cog_deg += 360 (line 38-41), and the header contracts COG in [0,360). test_track_output.cpp only exercises due-east (COG 90) and due-north (COG 0) velocities — both non-negative before wrap. No test uses a velocity with negative east or negative north (COG in (180,360)) that actually triggers the +=360 branch.
findings.why_it_matters: Half the compass (any south/west-going track) is unverified for the documented [0,360) contract. A sign error or a dropped wrap would emit a negative or out-of-range COG for those headings, feeding operators/downstream a wrong course, and no test catches it.
findings.suggested_test: Add cases: v_enu=(-10,0) expects COG 270; v_enu=(0,-10) expects COG 180; v_enu=(-10,-10) expects COG 225 — asserting each is in [0,360).

findings.title: Non-positive interval (clock skew) and multi-surveillance p_D aggregation untested
findings.description: evaluate() returns an empty MissOpportunity when dt<=0 (now<=last_checked, line 38-39) and, across multiple Surveillance profiles that both fire, keeps the strongest p_D (line 47-48). test_declared_sensor_activity.cpp covers single-channel surveillance miss/in-range/mid-sweep/sector in-and-out and a single cooperative-overdue, but never passes now<=last_checked, and never configures two surveillance channels to check p_D is the max.
findings.why_it_matters: Clock skew / out-of-order timestamps is an explicit scenario in CLAUDE.md's testing expectations. If the dt<=0 guard regressed, a backwards time step would charge a spurious surveillance miss (a false 'should have detected' weighing a non-detection). The p_D max-aggregation, if it regressed to last-wins or first-wins, would silently mis-weigh non-detections when several sensors overlap.
findings.suggested_test: Assert evaluate() with now<=last_checked returns surveillance_miss=false, cooperative_overdue=false, p_D=0. Separately, configure two overlapping surveillance profiles with p_D 0.6 and 0.9 covering the same track over a completed sweep and assert out.p_D==0.9.

units.coverage_notes:
Overall this scope is unusually well tested; the findings above are edges, not holes in core behavior.

WELL COVERED (verified by reading the test files):
- Cpa/computeCpaWithUncertainty (test_cpa_uncertainty.cpp): zero-covariance->zero-sigma, head-on position-sigma propagation, perpendicular-pass linear sigma scaling, parallel-velocity branch (sigma_tcpa=inf, sigma_cpa from dp cov), past-CPA/diverging branch, head-on near-zero-cpa isotropic fallback, probability-vs-Gaussian sweep, monotonicity in sigma. Zero-relative-velocity CPA is covered here.
- CpaEvaluator: no-own-ship no-op, Entered on high risk, no-refire-while-risky, track-deleted-while-Entered -> Exited (DeletedTrackFiresExited), emit_updates gating, Tentative skipped by default. The probability-driven Exit on a receding target IS covered end-to-end by tests/integration/test_full_stack_pipeline.cpp (head-on pass, deleted.size()==0 so the Exit is via probability, not deletion) — only the band-WIDTH distinction (finding 1) is unverified.
- synthesizeOwnShipTrack: ENU placement, position cov, valid/invalid velocity cov (test_cpa_synthesize_own_ship.cpp).
- TrackOutput: NED covariance NUMERICALLY asserted incl. high-latitude convergence-angle rotation with exact expected R and off-diagonal (CovarianceRotatedAtHighLatitude); SOG/COG for E and N; stationary singularity; velocity_observed gate; zero-vel-cov / 2D-state invalid velocity; covariance_is_default forwarding both ways.
- LiveOccupancyModel: extremely thorough — conservation invariant (SuppressionImpliesAnEmittedHazardCoversIt), datum recenter re-anchor (DatumRecenterKeepsGeographicSuppression), negative-coordinate cells (compA/compB at -1012.5 exercise std::floor cellOf), coverage-aware decay (in/out of range and sector), shadow-guard monotonicity on fixed inputs, corroboration veto A/B + anchored window, camera eviction/flicker/self-heal, clutter-adaptive bar, bounded-latency recovery, determinism.
- ShadowMask: wrap-through-pi occluder as one cluster (BearingWaparoundIsOneOccluder), weak-cluster no-shadow, wedge width from extent, multiple occluders, range margin, disabled. (Note: ShadowMask uses ENU math bearings so its wrap is at +/-pi = west, not north; wrap is covered.)
- StaticObstacleModel: ramp values numerically (inside/just-outside/mid/beyond), soft_max clamp both directions, datum recenter rebuild.
- StaticHazardEvaluator: enter/exit hysteresis, null-sink, stays-outside, co-located independent hysteresis; staticHazardId source_id disambiguation.
- BearingWedgeModel: claim = per-drain suppression not deletion (ADR-0002 never-invisible), datum-stable apex, contact-reuse/suspect fresh id, half-width floor, bounded range, prune-stale, math->true bearing.
- Coastline: inside/outside/waterline~0.5/monotonic-across-shore/empty/point-in-hole-is-water.
- ADR-0002 'never neither' end-to-end: tests/integration/test_static_obstacle_pipeline.cpp (phantom in footprint suppressed while real vessel in buffer still tracked + keep-clear alarm) plus the occupancy conservation-invariant unit test.

COULD NOT DETERMINE / minor: the CpaEvaluator 'dropped' Exited path (lines 66-79) emits an empty CpaPrediction (cpa_distance_m=0) and iterates a std::unordered_set to build the dropped list — the order of Exited events across multiple simultaneously-dropped tracks is hash-order dependent; whether any determinism/replay test compares the collision-event stream ordering was not confirmed (likely not part of the track-output determinism test, so low risk). The empty-payload contents of the deleted-track Exited event are not asserted anywhere.

findings.title: AIS SOG=1023 / COG=3600 velocity sentinels never fed to AisAdapter
findings.description: The velocity path guards `has_speed = ... *r.sog_knots < 1023.0` (line 37-38) and `has_course = ... *r.cog_deg < 360.0` (line 39-40) are the ONLY thing stopping AIS 'not available' sentinel values from becoming PositionVelocity2D measurement content. I searched the whole test tree for `1023` and `3600` (grep across tests/, excluding the venv) — the only hits are unrelated occupancy-grid coordinates. tests/adapters/ais/test_ais_adapter.cpp exercises heading 511, nav_status 15, and lat91/lon181, but never feeds sog_knots=1023 or cog_deg=3600. The .hpp contract explicitly promises these are dropped.
findings.why_it_matters: If the `< 1023.0` guard regresses (e.g. changed to `<=`, or dropped when refactoring the #20 velocity block), a raw SOG sentinel of 1023 knots is converted at line 41 to ~526 m/s and emitted as PositionVelocity2D velocity content, which corrupts the target's velocity estimate on the very next update — a silently wrong track. Consumers whose AIS decoder passes the raw sentinel (the contract says the adapter drops it) are exactly the exposed case, and no test would catch the regression.
findings.suggested_test: Feed a report with sog_knots=1023.0 (and separately cog_deg=3600.0) above the position path and assert the emitted measurement is Position2D with value.size()==2 — i.e. the sentinel is not turned into velocity content.

verdict.reasoning:
The claimed gap is real. AisAdapter.cpp lines 37-40 hold the only guards (`*r.sog_knots < 1023.0`, `*r.cog_deg < 360.0`) stopping AIS velocity "not available" sentinels from entering PositionVelocity2D content, and AisAdapter.hpp lines 33-34 document these as a consumer contract ("1023 sentinel dropped", "3600 sentinel").

I searched for real coverage and found none: (1) `grep -rn '1023\|3600\|sog_knots\|cog_deg' tests/ --include=*.cpp` (venv excluded) — the only `3600` hit is tests/static/test_live_occupancy_model.cpp:809, an unrelated occupancy-grid coordinate; no `1023` hit exists anywhere in tests. Every sog_knots/cog_deg value in the whole test tree is valid (10.0, 0.2, 2.0, 90.0, 123.0), none at/above the guards. (2) I read tests/adapters/ais/test_ais_adapter.cpp in full: it exercises lat91/lon181, NaN, heading 511, nav_status 15, low-SOG fallback, and anchored/moored suppression — but never sog_knots≥1023 or cog_deg≥360. (3) I read tests/replay/test_ais_csv_replay_adapter.cpp: valid SOG/COG only.

No indirect coverage: the DropsSentinelAndNaNPositions test bails at line 23 (position plausibility) before the velocity block, so a regression of `< 1023.0` to `<=`/removal during a #20 refactor would leave every test green while a raw SOG sentinel becomes ~526 m/s emitted velocity, corrupting the target's velocity on the next update. The failure mode (silently wrong track) is exactly the exposed case for a consumer whose decoder passes the raw sentinel the contract promises to drop. Notably the test author covered the heading-511 and nav_status-15 sentinels but omitted the SOG/COG sentinel pair.

findings.title: ISO-8601 / BaseDateTime timestamp parsing in loadAisCsv is entirely untested
findings.description: detectColumns (lines 60-61) advertises `basedatetime` / `timestamp` / `time` as time-column aliases — the MarineCadastre AIS export format — and parseTimeString has a full ISO-8601 branch (lines 93-115: sscanf date/time, fractional-seconds via strtod at the first '.', tm_mon = month-1 offset, timegm). I verified every AIS CSV fixture uses unix_time epoch floats (head of tests/fixtures/philos/out/ais_ferry_near/ais.csv = `unix_time,...` with `1667846694.361521`), and grep for `basedatetime`/ISO date patterns / `timegm` / `parseTimeString` across tests found nothing. The replay tests (test_philos_ospa, test_philos_farcross) call loadAisCsv but only on unix_time fixtures, so only the epoch branch runs.
findings.why_it_matters: The ISO branch is a documented, supported input surface but has zero coverage. A regression in it (month/day off-by-one from the tm_mon-1 handling, fractional-second capture picking the wrong dot, timegm-vs-mktime timezone drift, or a `+02:00` offset silently treated as UTC) yields wrong measurement timestamps for a standard AIS export — every track's timing and every OSPA/GOSPA number computed from that replay is silently wrong, and nothing catches it.
findings.suggested_test: Write a temp CSV with a `basedatetime` column holding `2020-03-01T00:00:01.250Z` rows and assert the parsed Timestamp equals the expected epoch (including the .250 fraction and correct month), plus a mixed epoch/ISO determinism check.

verdict.reasoning: The ISO-8601/BaseDateTime branch of parseTimeString (AisCsvReplayAdapter.cpp:93-116) and the basedatetime/timestamp/time column aliases (lines 60-61) are genuinely untested. I read the only dedicated test file, tests/replay/test_ais_csv_replay_adapter.cpp, in full: all 8 tests use CSVs with `unix_time,...` headers and epoch-float time values, exercising only the epoch branch (lines 86-91); none contains an ISO date, `basedatetime`, `timestamp`, or `time` column. parseTimeString is in an anonymous namespace and cannot be unit-tested directly — the only route is loadAisCsv with an ISO time column, which no test provides. Searches confirm no coverage: `grep -rni basedatetime` in tests/ (excluding readmes) = nothing; the only ISO-format date strings in non-fixture tests are test_csv_reader.cpp:13 / test_csv_writer.cpp:12 (`started_at_utc` benchmark run-metadata, unrelated to loadAisCsv). I enumerated every ais*.csv fixture header under tests/fixtures and data — all are unix_time, so the replay/scenario tests that call loadAisCsv (test_philos_ospa:155, test_philos_farcross:85/129, benchmark runners) also only hit the epoch branch; no integration path drives the ISO branch. The header (AisCsvReplayAdapter.hpp:19) documents MarineCadastre BaseDateTime as a supported surface, so this is not dead code. A regression in the tm_mon-1 month handling, fractional-second dot selection, or timegm timezone treatment (e.g. a `+02:00` offset silently taken as UTC) would produce wrong measurement timestamps and wrong OSPA/GOSPA for a standard AIS export, and no current test would catch it. The auditor did not miss existing coverage.

findings.title: Name-based CSV column detection never tested with a reordered/aliased header
findings.description: loadAisCsv (detectColumns, lines 56-78) and loadOwnshipCsv (adapters/replay/OwnshipCsvReader.cpp lines 48-56) map columns by header name so column order is supposed to be irrelevant. Every unit test and every fixture uses the single canonical order `unix_time,mmsi,lat,lon,sog_mps,cog_deg,nav_status,name` (see all CSV literals in test_ais_csv_replay_adapter.cpp). No test presents lat/lon (or time) in a non-canonical position, and there is no dedicated OwnshipCsvReader unit test at all — it is only reached through fixed-order fixtures.
findings.why_it_matters: The scope explicitly calls out header-reorder robustness. Because the detection is exercised with only one layout, a regression to positional indexing, or a broken alias, would pass the whole suite green while silently mis-mapping a real reordered export (e.g. reading longitude out of the latitude slot) — producing wrong ENU positions with no failing test.
findings.suggested_test: Load a CSV whose header is `lon,lat,mmsi,unix_time` (columns physically reordered) and assert the parsed position matches the canonical-order load of the same data; add an equivalent reordered-header test for loadOwnshipCsv.

findings.title: TTM speed/course unit variants K, S and relative-course 'R' unexercised
findings.description: distanceToMeters/speedToMps handle N/K/S codes (lines 29-43) and TTM course units 'R' add own heading (line 169). Grepping every RATTM literal in the tests shows units field is only ever N or K, speed>0 is fed only with N units, and course units are always 'T'. So speedToMps('K')/speedToMps('S') with nonzero speed, distanceToMeters('S'), and the relative-course->true 'R' branch are never executed.
findings.why_it_matters: These feed only the one-shot birth-velocity prior and the target-swap diagnostic (not recurring measurement content), so impact is bounded, but a wrong km/h, mph, statute-mile, or relative-course conversion would silently seed a birth velocity in the wrong magnitude/direction for radars that report those units, and no test constrains it.
findings.suggested_test: Add TTM cases with dist/speed units 'K' and 'S' (nonzero speed) asserting the birth_velocity_enu magnitude, and one with course units 'R' asserting the true-course composition against own heading.

units.coverage_notes: WELL COVERED (good news for synthesis): EdgeValidation is exhaustively tested both sides of every bound (lat/lon 90/180, NaN/Inf, range zero/negative/NaN/Inf, finite guard) in tests/adapters/util/test_edge_validation.cpp. AisAdapter: origin/covariance, sentinel+NaN position drop with valid interleaved fix, high/low-accuracy sigma, velocity emit above threshold (non-degenerate 4x4), low-SOG fallback, disable toggle, heading-511/nav-status-15 sentinel drop, anchored(1)/moored(5) velocity suppression vs underway(0) emit. AisCsvReplay: default-off byte-identical, opt-in velocity, low-SOG fallback, nav_status surface+15 drop, missing sog/cog columns tolerated, shared-helper no-drift proof against the NMEA path, anchored kinematic-identity ON-vs-OFF. ArpaAdapter: TLL position + out-of-range reject, TTM non-positive-range reject + position, published/unpublished/absent bias equivalence, config heading-std + per-pose heading floor/clamp (#16), GPS-std inflation, TLL-unaffected-by-GPS, birth-velocity seed + zero-speed no-seed, swap-diagnostic course-jump threshold. Static obstacles (adapters/static): extremely thorough — full/sparse attributes, non-Point/null skip, malformed JSON -> runtime_error, per-feature skip keeps following valid feature, out-of-range lat AND lon both sides, non-numeric coord skip, negative-radius clamp on all three radii, missing-file throw. Coastline (tests/land): Polygon + MultiPolygon + on-disk Boston fixture; [lon,lat] order verified via inside/outside prior. OwnShipNmeaAdapter: GGA position, HDT heading, malformed reject, HDOP*UERE, absent-HDOP-stays-zero, adaptive-UERE window publish/dominate/maneuver-fallback, RMC SOG/COG->ENU + zero-SOG sigma, RMC precedence/stale/estimator-fallback (multi-heading and bias-dispatch have their own files). RemoteTrackAdapter: covariance inflate/default, edge reject, per-track/per-station thinning, velocity opt-in on/off/default, circular-AIS MMSI guard. CameraBearingCsvReader: heading composition, wrap across north, no-pose drop, invalid-sigma reject, per-camera source ids. Replay loaders (AIS/plot/radar-truth/camera/ownship) all std::sort their output by time, so unsorted-input tolerance is structurally present and radar-truth has an explicit monotonic-after-sort assertion. NMEA parse: valid/bad-checksum/missing-delimiter covered. COULD NOT DETERMINE: did not execute the suite, so fixture-gated replay tests' actual run/skip status is unverified (fixtures ARE present in this main tree, so they should run). UNCERTAIN LOW: GeoJsonCoastline addPolygon parses interior rings (holes) but no adapter/coastline test confirms a point inside a hole is excluded from the land prior (a hole bug would suppress births over enclosed water); this straddles core CoastlineGeometry so I did not raise it as an adapter finding.

findings.title: No test verifies emitter-injected noise magnitude or its self-consistency with adapter-stamped R (NEES/NIS foundation)
findings.description: Every emitter UNIT test (test_ais_emitter, test_arpa_emitter, test_eoir_emitter, test_own_ship_emitter) runs with pos_std_m / range_std_m / bearing_std_deg / gps_pos_std_m == 0 (noise OFF). The one covariance test (EoIrEmitter.BearingOnlyModeWidensRangeCovariance) checks the R *shape* on a noise-free measurement. The noisy paths are exercised only by the bus/GPS sweeps (test_bus_gps_sweep, test_bus_heading_sweep, test_bus_adaptive_uere), which mostly print tables and SUCCEED(); test_bus_regression asserts only a loose one-sided OSPA-ratio upper bound (baseline*~5, with 15% headroom). Nothing asserts that when you request std=X the empirical spread of emitted values around truth is X, nor that the emitter's injected std equals the covariance the adapter stamps on the resulting Measurement. The NEES/NIS consistency arm (alpha_hat, coverage_95) is validated in test_consistency only against hand-crafted IID-Gaussian InnovationEvents/BenchResult, never against the real emitter->adapter->tracker path; test_sweep only counts NIS/NEES rows.
findings.why_it_matters: The sim is the PRIMARY controlled eval gate and the user's stated purpose is co-evaluating estimators/associators. A subtle noise bug (std treated as variance, noise on one axis only, wrong unit deg-vs-rad, or seed not advanced) would silently bias OSPA/RMSE and — worse — the alpha_hat/coverage calibration verdict in every noisy sweep, with no red test. Because alpha_hat only means 'filter mistuning' if the sim is self-consistent (injected std == adapter R), an undetected mismatch makes the consistency arm measure the harness, not the filter, and can flip an A/B estimator comparison.
findings.suggested_test: Add a statistical test: run one emitter (or the SimulatedSensorBus) with a known std>0 over N>=2000 fixed-truth draws, assert the empirical residual std/covariance matches the requested std within a chi-square-sized tolerance; and a self-consistency test that drives the full emitter->adapter->tracker->NisCollector/computeNees path on a stationary/CV target and asserts alpha_hat approx 1 and coverage_95 approx 0.95 when emitter std == advertised R (e.g. own-ship report_gps_std=true).

findings.title: No 'different seed -> different noise' test for the sensor emitters
findings.description: Emitter determinism is covered (test_bus_determinism: same seed -> identical output), and SkewInjector has an explicit different-seeds-diverge test. But the AIS/ARPA/EO-IR/own-ship emitters have no test that two different seeds actually produce different noise realizations. A same-seed determinism test passes even if the seed is ignored entirely (e.g. rng_ constructed from a constant), so a dropped/hard-coded seed in an emitter would leave all seeds producing identical 'noise', silently collapsing the multi-seed sweep's variance estimate (AggStats std_ospa) to near-zero.
findings.why_it_matters: Multi-seed sweeps report mean +/- std across seeds to judge whether an estimator delta is real. If a seed-plumbing bug makes every seed identical, the reported spread is fake and marginal A/B wins look significant when they are not.
findings.suggested_test: For each emitter, run seed A vs seed B with std>0 on identical truth and assert the emitted value streams differ.

findings.title: countIdSwitches silently ignores targets that appear after the first step
findings.description: countIdSwitches fixes k = steps.front().truth.size() and both allocates last(k) and loops `i < s.truth.size() && i < k`, slot-keying by index. Any truth slot beyond the step-0 cardinality is never scored, and if truth slot order varies per step the tally mixes physical targets. This is the headline id-switch metric consumed by the bus comparison tests (countIdSwitches(r.steps, cutoff) in BusComparisonHelpers and several test_bus_*). The tests exercise only fixed-cardinality-from-t0 scenarios; there is no test with a target appearing mid-scenario, and no test with per-step slot reordering. (The benchmark-side computeContinuity is correctly truth_id-keyed and IS tested for both cases, so the safe path exists — but the scenario path used by the bus suite is not guarded.)
findings.why_it_matters: A scenario where a non-cooperative target appears after t0 (a named test scenario in CLAUDE.md) would under-report id switches for that target, so an estimator that churns identity on late arrivals scores artificially clean in the bus comparisons.
findings.suggested_test: Add a countIdSwitches test with a target that appears at step 1 (growing cardinality) and one with per-step slot reordering, asserting switches are counted for the late/ reordered target.

units.coverage_notes:
Scope covered: core/scenario/{Ospa,Gospa,TGospa,Metrics,TruthResample}, core/benchmark/{Metrics,Consistency,ExistenceLabel,Sweep,Config}, sim/{AisEmitter,ArpaEmitter,EoIrEmitter,OwnShipEmitter,NmeaEncode,SkewInjector} and matching tests in tests/{scenario,benchmark,sim}.

WELL COVERED (searched by symbol + scenario keyword + asserted-constant):
- Metric kernels vs hand-computed values are excellent. Ospa: empty/single/two-pairs/clip + optimal-vs-greedy (test_ospa). Gospa scalar AND GospaComponents decomposition (localization/missed/false_/n_missed/n_false, alpha=1, optimal-assignment) hand-checked (test_gospa). TGospa: per-scan accumulation, switch penalty on assignment flip, all-missed/all-false cardinality (test_tgospa). benchmark id_switches/continuity/RMSE hand-checked incl. truth_id-keying vs slot reorder, time-varying cardinality, per-truth breakdown, and COG 90-deg wrap (test_metrics benchmark). countIdSwitches scenario-side: no-switch/one-switch/track-loss (test_metrics scenario).
- TruthResample: linear position interp + finite-diff velocity, gap>max_gap breaks presence, single-fix nearest-tick, async targets share ticks, sorted+tick-contiguous determinism, empty/disabled (test_truth_resample). NOTE the flagged 'heading wrap' concern does NOT apply: TruthSample carries a velocity VECTOR (no heading angle is ever angularly interpolated), so there is no wrap hazard here.
- Consistency NEES/NIS dof: m=1 and m=2 IID-Gaussian band tests, fitted-alpha at alpha in {0.5,1,4}, singular-S drop accounting, below-min band suppression, NEES-from-known-filter mean approx 2 (test_consistency). Wilson-Hilferty and exact chi2_1/chi2_2 one-sided quantiles are used and exercised.
- ExistenceLabel timing + parsing: windowed/whole-clip/embedded-semicolons/notes/KEEP_MIXED/Unknown-default, activeAtUnix before/inside/after + whole-clip, and two committed fixtures (test_existence_label).
- NmeaEncode round-trip: ddmm formatting incl. minute-carry, checksum wrap parses via real parseNmea (test_nmea_encode). Crucially the SEMANTIC encode->parse->value round-trip IS covered end-to-end elsewhere: ArpaEmitter RATTM -> ArpaAdapter -> Measurement value near truth (test_arpa_emitter ProducesMeasurementNearTruthInEnu), and GGA -> OwnShipProvider lat/lon within 1e-6 (test_own_ship_emitter). AIS/EO-IR emitters feed structured reports (no NMEA encode).
- SkewInjector (clock skew): zero-profile identity, truth-timestamp preservation, same-seed determinism, different-seed reorder, single-sensor stable order, empty input, RemoteTrack indexability (test_skew_injector).
- Sweep/BenchRunner config plumbing: the observable-output knobs ARE guarded end-to-end through runSweep by A/B tests that assert occ_* rows and cross-arm deltas (test_occupancy_ab, test_occupancy_detector_gates, test_los_guard_haxr_ab, test_veto_isolation_*), plus detectionModelFor per-sensor/source/clutter-map table plumbing and Config factory freshness/distinct-instance guards (test_sweep, test_config). Config OccupancyDetectorArmsDifferOnlyInCoverageFlag guards A/B validity of one knob.

COULD NOT FULLY DETERMINE: Config knobs that produce NO distinct output row and are not part of an A/B delta (e.g. use_sensor_activity_model / DeclaredSensorActivity wiring, and whether specific pmbm_config()/mht_config() field values provably change tracker behavior through runSweep rather than just being constructed) are only indirectly guarded by A/B configs that happen to differ. A silent failure to apply such a knob whose two arms otherwise coincide would not necessarily be caught, but I could not construct a concrete wrong-metric scenario without deeper PMBM-side tracing (out of this unit's source scope), so it is not reported as a finding.
